prompt: Our SaaS company provides B2B cloud-based HR software. We have 75 employees and €15M annual revenue. A customer just told us we need to be NIS2 compliant. Are we directly subject to NIS2, and if so what do we need to do to comply with Article 21?
assertions: Identifies that a B2B cloud SaaS provider may fall under NIS2 as a managed service provider (MSP) / ICT service management (B2B) under Annex I, or as a cloud computing service provider under Annex II digital providers
Confirms size threshold is met (75 employees ≥ 50; €15M ≥ €10M) making the entity in scope
Explains all 10 Art. 21 measures or provides detailed practical guidance on the most relevant ones for a SaaS company (incident handling, secure SDLC, supply chain, MFA)
Addresses Art. 23 incident reporting obligations and explains what constitutes a significant incident for a cloud/SaaS provider
Differentiates between direct NIS2 obligations and being included as a supplier in a customer's NIS2 supply chain risk assessment (Art. 21(2)(d) / Art. 26)
